summary: Tier 1: strip '^Sacred Texts ... Previous Next' nav prefix (the ~30% win) + re-chunk/re-embed
description:
Tier 1 / the 80/20: strip the leading site-nav prefix from chunk bodies.

Pattern (collection word varies — Classics / Buddhism / Zoroastrianism / Egypt / Esoteric):
    ^Sacred Texts\b.*?\bPrevious\s+Next\b\s*      (non-greedy)
971 local chunks (30%) match. Add it to pre_strip_patterns in every chunking
config whose text has nav-prefixed chunks. By tradition (local counts):
neoplatonism=716 (plotinus-select-works-index — page-as-chunk, currently NO
pre_strip), taoism=137, egyptian=68, zoroastrianism=19, christian_mysticism=14,
greek_mystery=10, jewish_mysticism=4, hermeticism=2, buddhism=1 (diamond-sutra).
Enumerate the exact text_ids by re-running the corroboration scan.

Mechanism notes:
- pre_strip is applied PER-PAGE for page-as-chunk/multi sources and whole-file
  for single sources; both anchor "^", so each page's/file's leading nav is
  removed. The round-trip test mirrors this exactly (tests/test_chunking.py
  _load_raw_text), so it will stay valid.
- Re-chunk affected texts, then refresh local guru.db: bootstrap_chunks (upsert
  node labels) + cleanup_stale_chunk_nodes --apply (if chunk counts shift) +
  embed_corpus --tradition X (INSERT OR REPLACE refreshes vectors) — the exact
  sequence used in todo:73c83a22.

Done contract:
- A scan reports 0 chunks whose body starts with the nav prefix.
- tests/test_chunking.py round-trip stays green.
- Local guru.db has no stale chunk_ids for affected texts; embeddings refreshed.
- Regression test: assert no chunk body matches ^Sacred Texts\b.*Previous\s+Next.

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content: Validated nav-strip pattern: ^Sacred Texts\b[A-Za-z ]*?\bIndex(?:\s+Index)?(?:\s+(?:Previous|Next))+\s+ — strips all 981 affected chunk bodies cleanly with 0 false-positives on the other ~2165. Implementation deviation from spec: the nav is a UNIFORM sacred-texts.com artifact spanning 32 configs (collection word varies: Classics/Taoism/Egypt/Egypt EHH Index/Zoroastrianism/Esoteric/Judaism/Gnosticism and Hermetica/Buddhism), so instead of pasting the identical pattern into 32 TOML files (error-prone, loses comments under tomli_w rewrite, misses future sacred-texts sources), add it ONCE as a BASELINE_PRE_STRIP in scripts/chunk.py applied to every text before per-config pre_strip_patterns (no-op for non-sacred-texts; 0 false-positives confirmed). Round-trip test updated to mirror the baseline. Operates on existing raw (re-chunk only, no re-acquire). This is the doc's caveat-#4 'handle the source artifact centrally' done at chunker level.

resolution.note: Stripped the sacred-texts nav prefix from 981 chunks across 32 texts -> 0, via BASELINE_PRE_STRIP in chunk.py (one change, not 32 config edits; covers future sacred-texts sources). Pattern is non-^-anchored (catches interior navs in multi-section pages), letters-only + non-greedy, 0 false-positives. Re-chunked the 32, refreshed guru.db (3137 files=nodes=embeddings, 0 stale). Recreated chunking/zoroastrianism/bundahishn.toml (lost config — pre-existing anomaly). Commit 10ed3a2; tests 263 green. FOLLOW-UPS (flagged, not done): (1) re-chunk shifted boundaries for 3 sub-split texts (plotinus 838->831, egyptian-book-of-the-dead 358->357, zhuangzi 295->294); embeddings refreshed but their promoted EXPRESSES tags may need re-tagging. (2) 'Buy this Book at Amazon.com' front-matter artifact in 21 chunks — separate pattern, fold into C2 (0a708fa4). (3) enoch-charles-1917 + tertium-organum show pre-existing chunker drift (re-chunk != committed) unrelated to nav — NOT touched; separate issue. Downstream: guru-web PG re-export is C5 (829993fd).
